what_it_does: It is a package-level docstring that describes voice/ears as a set of GPU speech-to-text HTTP services (parakeet, canary, granite), each running on its own port and in its own virtual environment because of conflicting NeMo/transformers dependencies; it points readers to AGENTS.md and compares the package to voice/engines/.
observations: The file contains only a module docstring — no imports, functions, classes, or executable code.
It names three STT model families: parakeet, canary, and granite.
It states each model is exposed as an independent HTTP service on its own port.
It states each model has its own venv due to dependency conflicts between NeMo and transformers.
It explicitly calls itself the STT twin of voice/engines/.
It references AGENTS.md for more context.
standouts: Package marker whose only payload is architectural intent, not code.
Strong isolation story: one port + one venv per model.
conventions: Uses __init__.py as a package README / subsystem map.
Follows the convention of a leading module docstring explaining directory purpose.
Does not export any symbols from the package.
concerns: No __all__ or public API surface is declared.
The file references AGENTS.md but gives no hint about its location or contents.
No runtime metadata (versions, ports, health endpoints) is present.
notes: This file is pure orientation: it tells you voice/ears is a fleet of isolated GPU STT services, not a shared library.
questions: What are the exact ports and service boundaries for parakeet, canary, and granite?
How are the per-model virtual environments created and managed?
What specific dependency conflicts require isolation?
What does AGENTS.md prescribe for this package?
What is the exact relationship or shared infrastructure with voice/engines/?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to document the voice/ears package as an isolated GPU STT service fleet, and the file does exactly that through its docstring with no executable code.
apparent_themes: Dependency isolation through separate virtual environments.
intention_signals: It is trying to establish voice/ears as the STT counterpart to voice/engines/, framing the directory as a set of independently deployable, dependency-isolated HTTP STT services and directing readers to AGENTS.md for the broader agent design.
novelty: It treats __init__.py as a high-level subsystem manifest rather than an import/export hub, and it names concrete model families directly in the package docstring.
contribution: It contributes the package-level intent, scope, and boundary description for the ears STT subsystem, including the per-model isolation rationale, the list of model families, the HTTP-service framing, and a pointer to AGENTS.md.
summary_for_embedding: voice/ears/__init__.py is a package docstring that frames the directory as a set of isolated GPU speech-to-text HTTP services (parakeet, canary, granite), each with its own port and virtual environment, analogous to voice/engines/.
intention_for_embedding: It was meant to orient readers to the ears STT subsystem and its per-model service and virtual-environment isolation strategy.
